review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Office politics -There is extreme politics at branch level by seniors to retain their positions and entry level freshers have the hardest time of their life to deal with such seniors and learn to work simultaneously,the colleagues are not supportive at all .They won't help you out in anyway .
Moreover , the seniors would conspire against a fresher to put the entire blame on them as in why they are lacking in business and tranfer them 1700kms away from hometown .
2.High pressure of sales target .No matter how much you can achieve in a month you ' ll always be yelled at .
3.Ineffective HR- No matter how many times you approach the HR asking for a resolution to your problem they don't listen and ignore your request .
4.Deaf senior management - The senior management Is not concerned with whatever the issues the employees are facing .They won't move a finger .
5.PO batch Is just a business model where they are just generating more and more workers who will work in their branches at low salaries and they are entitled to throw them at any part of the country without considering them as humans .Also entrapping them in excessive loan cycle so that they don't leave before 2-3 years but unfortunately not everybody gets a good boss or posting so even after bearing this torture for extended period of time till they can ,they might have to leave the organisation.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Valuable Learning Experience: Working at the bank provided an invaluable learning experience. I gained a comprehensive understanding of the financial industry and its operations. Supportive Team: The colleagues and supervisors were incredibly supportive and willing to share their knowledge. This made the work environment more collaborative and conducive to growth. Professional Development: The bank prioritized professional development, offering various training programs and opportunities for skill enhancement. This focus on growth was encouraging. Responsibility and Trust: Despite being an intern, I was entrusted with meaningful tasks. The trust and responsibility given to me contributed significantly to my professional development. Networking: The bank provided ample opportunities for networking within the financial industry. This exposure to professionals from different domains was enriching.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Workload at Times: There were instances of heavy workloads, especially during peak seasons. Balancing multiple responsibilities could be challenging. Pressure to Meet Targets: Meeting targets was essential, which sometimes led to a high-pressure environment. It required consistent performance.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: "Empowering Growth: A Management Trainee's Journey at ICICI Bank"
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Working as a Management Trainee at ICICI Bank for a year can offer several advantages:

Comprehensive Training: ICICI Bank is known for its structured and thorough training programs. As a Management Trainee, you can expect to receive extensive training in various aspects of banking and financial services, providing you with a strong foundation for your career.

Diverse Exposure: The bank's wide range of services, including retail banking, corporate banking, and financial markets, offers a diverse and comprehensive exposure. This exposure can be valuable for your professional development, allowing you to explore different areas within the banking sector.

Networking Opportunities: Working at ICICI Bank provides you with the chance to build a professional network within the industry. Engaging with experienced professionals and colleagues during your training can open doors to future opportunities and collaborations.

Career Advancement: Completing the Management Trainee program successfully may open doors to various career advancement opportunities within ICICI Bank. The experience gained during the training period can serve as a solid foundation for future roles with more responsibilities.

Skill Development: The intensive nature of the training program is designed to enhance your skills, not only in banking but also in areas such as communication, problem-solving, and teamwork. These skills are transferable and can be beneficial in any professional setting.

Industry Insight: Working at ICICI Bank allows you to gain insights into the trends and dynamics of the banking and financial industry. This knowledge is crucial for staying updated on industry best practices and understanding the factors that influence the financial sector.

Brand Reputation: ICICI Bank is a well-established and reputable financial institution. Having the bank's name on your resume can enhance your professional credibility and open doors to opportunities beyond your immediate role.

Performance Recognition: If you excel during your one-year tenure, there may be opportunities for performance recognition, such as promotions or additional responsibilities. ICICI Bank often values and rewards high-performing employees.

It's important to note that individual experiences may vary, and the level of satisfaction in any job role depends on personal preferences and career goals.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
While experiences can vary, here are some potential cons of working as a management trainee at ICICI Bank for one year:

High Workload and Pressure: Banking environments, including ICICI Bank, can be fast-paced and demanding. As a management trainee, you may experience a high workload and pressure to meet targets and deadlines.

Limited Exposure in One Year: One year might be a relatively short time to gain a comprehensive understanding of all aspects of banking and management. It could be challenging to get exposure to a wide range of functions within the organization.

Limited Autonomy: As a trainee, you may have limited decision-making authority or autonomy. This could be frustrating if you are eager to take on more responsibilities and make significant contributions.

Uncertain Job Placement Post-Training: While management training programs often lead to permanent positions, there may be uncertainty about the specific role or department you will be placed in after completing the program.

Potential for Routine Tasks: In some cases, management trainees may be assigned routine tasks that might not fully utilize their skills or provide a diverse learning experience.

Challenging Work-Life Balance: The banking industry is known for long working hours, and as a management trainee, you might find it challenging to maintain a healthy work-life balance.

Adaptation to Corporate Culture: Adapting to the corporate culture of a large organization like ICICI Bank can be challenging, especially for those who are new to the industry or have different prior experiences.

It's essential to note that these potential cons can vary based on individual experiences and the specific details of the management trainee program at ICICI Bank. It's advisable to research the program thoroughly, speak to current or former trainees, and consider your own preferences and career goals before making a decision.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Professional Growth Competitive Salary and Benefits Training and Developmen Exposure to a Diverse Range of Customers Job Stability:
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Workload and Stress: As a Collection Manager, you may be responsible for managing a large portfolio of delinquent accounts and overseeing a team of collection agents. This can lead to a high workload and stress levels, particularly during peak collection periods. Target-Driven Environment: ICICI Bank has a target-driven work culture, which can be challenging for some employees. As a Collection Manager, you may be expected to meet monthly and quarterly collection targets, which can add pressure and stress to your work. Customer Complaints: Debt collection is often perceived negatively by customers, and collection agents and managers may face customer complaints and negative feedback. This can be emotionally challenging for some individuals. Time Management: As a Collection Manager, you may need to manage multiple tasks and competing priorities simultaneously. This requires strong time management skills and the ability to prioritize effectively. Regulatory Compliance: ICICI Bank is subject to various regulatory requirements related to debt collection, and Collection Managers need to ensure compliance with all applicable laws and regulations. This can add complexity and regulatory risk to the job.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: I was excited to join ICICI BANK along with 4-5 other new joinees. Indiction went well and we were introductions to our manager. They have lack of technological infrastructure and laptops to provide to the new joiness. The manager asked us to get a laptop on our from tech team without support. Tech team didn’t even care to respond. The manager ultimately started cursing, threatening to transfer to remote location, throw us out of our jobs, write a review that no one will hire us and use abusive language. He was very unprofessional and also wanted us to do all his dirty work while he wanted to himself clean. I was even asked to take responsibility of work delays from his side, even calling female employees from other teams at 10 pm knowing the fact that she was on leave. He had anger issues and he used to abuse almost every one in the team resulting in me witnessing 10-11 people leaving his team within a short span of 4 months. I requested HR to move me to some other team but she hardly responded and infact my manager somehow came to know about my complaint about him and he started taunting me more. My Mental health had already worsened enough that it was impacting my physical well being that I had to call my mom to stay with me. Hence I finally decided yo join back the organisation that I left and they were happy to take me back despite of having a no rehiring policy for 6 months of resignation. That 4 months at ICICI scared me for life and despite of what anyone would say, there are teams where this happens. I was a part of the MNC team in Bangalore back then and the manager was later promoted and given other department. I even sent mails to Hr head, my manager’s boss and his boss as well but no action was taken against him. It all seems very okay from outside but this is a true story that will always give me chills whenever I think about my time at ICICI.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: ICICI Bank Ltd is one of the leading private sector banks in India, and there are several pros associated with it: Strong financial position: ICICI Bank has a strong financial position, with a solid capital base, strong liquidity, and a diversified funding profile. Wide range of products and services: The bank offers a wide range of products and services, including personal and corporate banking, investment banking, and wealth management. Strong distribution network: ICICI Bank has a strong distribution network, with a large number of branches and ATMs across the country, as well as a strong digital presence. Strong customer base: The bank has a large and diversified customer base, including retail customers, corporate customers, and institutional customers. Strong focus on technology: ICICI Bank has a strong focus on technology and innovation, and has implemented several digital initiatives to enhance customer experience. International presence: ICICI Bank has a strong international presence, with branches and subsidiaries in several countries, providing customers with access to global banking services. Strong corporate governance: The bank has a strong corporate governance framework in place, which helps to ensure that the bank's operations are conducted in an ethical and transparent manner.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: While ICICI Bank is one of the leading private sector banks in India, there are also some cons associated with it. Here are a few examples: High Non-Performing Assets (NPA): ICICI Bank has been facing some issues with high Non-Performing Assets (NPA) in recent years, which is a measure of bad loans. This can have a negative impact on the bank's profitability and financial performance. Regulatory fines and penalties: ICICI Bank has been subject to regulatory fines and penalties for various compliance and regulatory violations in the past. Issues with corporate governance: There have been some issues with corporate governance at ICICI Bank in the past, including allegations of insider trading and conflict of interest. High-interest rates: ICICI Bank has been charging high-interest rates on loans and credit cards, which can be a disadvantage for customers. Issues with digital banking services: Some customers have reported issues with the bank's digital banking services, such as online transactions, mobile banking, and internet banking. Complaints about customer service: Some customers have reported poor customer service from the bank, with long wait times, unhelpful staff and lack of proper assistance. It's worth noting that some of the cons mentioned might be subject to change and not necessarily a general trend. ICICI Bank like any other financial institution has been undergoing changes and improvements, it's always good to stay updated and check the recent development of the bank.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: ICICI Bank is very competitive & expecting employees to give their best. Employees generally get hikes every year. Apart from salary, they can claim local conveyance for client visits.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
I served ICICI Bank around 9 years. Had never seen a day where work pressure is normal, it's always very high in all departments. Too much of office politics, it's very difficult to survive if you don't learn to live with it. Managers are very un-professional, they are always rude & treat staff very badly. Employees have to follow-up multiple times to get their leaves or any other requests approved by them. 

No learning opportunities are provided to employees except for self-financed management programs. HR also doesn't support employees, in fact a large of employees had been transferred last year to different states since they had raised voice and complained against their managers. Actually, ICICI bank had indirectly told those employees to leave the bank.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
I am a CA  and works as credit manager in loan department.  There are plenty of cons in all aspects working at this company.
1. The job profile does not have any correlation for CA qualification.  The work can be handled by any non CA. IF YOU HIRE A CA, YOU SHOULD KNOW HOW TO UTILIZE THEIR TALENT.
2. They will make you work till 2 AM during last two days of month end.  I don't see any necessity to do so. Totally inhuman. I am shocked to see the work culture where you are making lady employee work till such bad hours with having a lady CEO. 
3. For a fresher who is permanent employee, no leaves for one whole year.. She can't avail as they ll never approve. Even in worst case if they approve, that day salary is cut. What sort of ethics, culture is this. Even a peon is entitled for 1 day leave per month.
4. No tracker management, absolute chaos. it's a fish market. People come to you with issues, all at once. 
5.they will make you work till 2 am. And nobody cares about your dinner. Canteen is not open, manager doesn't care about ordering food. And if they order also they ll order a dosa..seriously..is this a corporate culture?
6. Sometimes even if you don't have work you are made to sit idle but not let go home.. Is this a school?

It was her first job. She has resigned a better company now.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Having built a book size a few hundred thousand crores,the bank provides a platform to learn from random events.A very secured work culture was displayed by the bank through the recession from 2008.The entire retail sales force was accomadated in various roles from operations to recoveries.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: The bank was in a tremendous growth phase during the early part of the last decade,employees were promoted every year and recruitment was round the clock.With the macro economy crashing in 2008,the bank was over capacitated and still accomodating people.Salary hikes were only a function of promotions in the bank throughout the decade.The bank had changed its stance on growth and was  stringent  .Though there were no salary cuts perse hikes were mimimal or zilch for 2 years  even for well rated employees.Promotion policy was changed from every year to once in 3 years as the bank has become risk averse suddenly which has translated into very poor salary by industry standards.In te process tha bank has procreated several thousands of demotivated employees.Roles have been downsized ,salaries have not been revised.While the bank makes profit every quarter due to cost cutting and credit quality(acheived by closing down several branches and products),the employee as a stakeholder is yet to feature in the management list.At ICICI bank employees don have access to internet beyond google search,also attachments>1 mb cannot be sent by most employees in key fucntions.The bank ahs a matrix for recruitment and promotion leaving no room for recogonising talent.

review-details_employee__5iA6v: Chief manager human resources, training and development department
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
You get to learn a lot since you need to engaged with control groups and regulators to get approval for new processes.The job role is fluid so you this is also an opportunity to learn about different facets of your function. 
The peer group is very knowledgeable.Automation & Digitization is the key differentiation with respect to other banks. Organisation believes in Meritocracy and not experience is not the only reason for projects or tasks landing in your tray.   
You can grow in the organization if you are committed to the companies cause.You need to align to organizational changes. The organisation allows you to take calls on a daily basis. However it also expects you to disclose any non compliance by escalating to seniors along with proper channels such as Audit department ,corporate communication team and regulators alike. You can engage with senior management at any time to bring to their attention any opportunities or threats you may encounter in your work area and they do listen to you. 
Organisation structure is tall so you will need to put in the years to reach the top brass.Additionally the organisation allows you to make honest mistakes and learn and rectify them.
Lots of training opportunities are given to you sharpen your skills required to do your job more effectively.

As most nine-to-fivers know, finding an excuse to sneak out of the office early takes just as much skill as landing a job in the first place.If you aren’t one of the lucky few with a job that offers “summer Fridays” or personal leave days, then your best bet for skipping out early is coming up with a host of solid, no-questions-asked excuses to leave work early.

Here are the best excuses sure to get you out of your cubicle and under the sun—or back into bed. May you ignore a guilty conscience, send convincing emails, and still ha ve your job the following day.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Acting like you just magically caught the flu is one of the oldest excuses in the book, though it’s not as convincing if you use it too often. But if you use this excuse sparingly around fever season it just might work in your favor. You could even blame it on the take-out from the sketchy restaurant you ate at the night before. Of course, if you’re going to use the sudden vomit excuse to grab a drink, going to the bar down the street from the office might not be the best idea. Better not to run the risk of your boss seeing you taking shots after saying your kid has the flu.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. A highly clerical job with no incentives. 
2. No structure in the department. Employees are switched from one profile to another in a span of 3 months. Zero stability at the ground level. I had a total of 4 reporting managers in a span of 1 year. 
3. No clarity in the organization. Today, the team is asked to focus and sell 1 product and the next day it's a different product altogether. Totally messed up.
4. From dealing with corporates, the focus shifted to opening savings account, home loans etc. No clarity on the profile. The work is at par with that of a PB/VB.
5. Every other day the top management asks for tons of data. One has to spend a lot of unproductive time in making it and the next day it has no value.
6. Profile not relevant for any Tier-1/Tier-2 B-school graduate.
7. During Covid, Company stopped paying salaries from Mid-June onwards stating that WFH will only be allowed if you are at your place of employment. Horrible HR policies.

To start with the cons ill tell you exactly and how things work here :

I joined the west cluster of Mumbai region as an RM Wealth , coming out of good MBA college you expect atleast a chair and a PC , but i will tell you , you have to stand like a sales boy in the offcie , cater to all the needs of your boss and especially the walk in customers . There is no pride in this job , you dress well and you will be depositing cash of the wealth clients and the list goes on till you start feeling what exactly are you doing here . 

The wealth management concept of ICICI bank prevails on bashing of the juniors and solely that only with of course hierarchy on point . The top guy abuses the lower guy and it keeps on happening till the DMII level which is so called the RM Wealth A.k.a sales boys of wealth department . 

There is no infrastructure , you have to use your own phone , or buy one if you dont have to cold call the customers to buy credit card , loans and what not , juts like a sales boy .

Leave alone sitting , if you seen sitting in the branch , you are abused infornt of the customers and everyone till you start hating yourslef only . The seniors so called branch managers and cluster branch managers have zero knowledge apart from abusing the juniors and acting cool after doing that .

Coming to the real job , RM wealth has to just do the service of the customers by travelling in auto rickshaw , local train because with the salary they give you , you cant afford to travel by uber. Small services like collecting pan card copy and all , from their homes to back to the branch is done by a wealth manager DMII. 

There is only one product in the ICICI bank amd that is life insurance (ULIPS).
Whole wealth management sells life insurance , also some times credit card , home loans which are subjected to pressure from the top. But LI is the product they are asked to sell because once the customer is trapped in the product , its a revenue generating product for a bank because of the closed ended scheme and premium payments .

Coming back to the culture , you are bashed every morning and every evening , the job is 9-9 job and you end up just sleeping at your home . 

The HR department knws everything and they play hire more game where they know people will leave but also job needy freshers will keep coming in .

Its a sad story of how dreams are crushed in name of wealth management and the bank ICICI does nothing for it employees . Diwali or new year or holi , you dont get a penny more and its just another day in the office .

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
One of the biggest advantages of Credit Card is that it gives the users easy access to credit. By using the card, you can purchase anything you want now and pay the amount later. The money used for the purchase does not go from the bank, thus it does not affect your savings.
If used well and if you are disciplined with your repayments, Credit Cards are the easiest and the efficient way to build your credit score and establish your creditworthiness. Based on your Credit Card usage and repayment history, the banks approve or disapprove your loan applications.
If you want to make a large purchase like an expensive phone or furniture without touching your savings, you can use your Credit Card for the purchase and repay it in EMIs. Paying through EMIs is much cheaper than opting for a Personal Loan as the interest rate for these loans can be as high as 20-25%.
Another significant credit features and benefits is that most cards come loaded with a wide range of incentives and offers like the reward points, cashbacks and air miles. Also, there are many retailers that offer special discount on purchase of products through Credit Cards, which helps you save a significant sum.
One of the lesser known benefits of having a Credit Card is that when you purchase anything with your card, all your transactions are recorded and the details of the same are sent to you every month through the Credit Card statement. You can use the statement to keep track of your expenses and chalk out your budget accordingly.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Credit Cards have many fees and taxes like late payment penalty, annual fees, processing fees, joining fees and renewal fees. Make sure that you do your research well and compare Credit Card rates before you sign up.
If you fail to pay your Credit Card dues within the due date, the debt is carried forward to the next month along with the interest on it. The interest is accrued over a period and the interest can be quite high.
Although there are plenty of security measures for online transactions, there is always a risk of being a Credit Card fraud victim. There are many fraudsters who use advanced technologies to clone a card and gain access to your Credit Card information, which can then be used for purchases. To avoid this, you must carefully check your monthly statement and look for any suspicious transactions and inform the authorities immediately about the same.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
In the garb  of meritocracy the Bank has created “a very toxic culture.” Employees are disheartened, trust is low, and a lot of people feel and behave like victims. For every two people, one person is looking for a job. There is an atmosphere of fear which has been created. The prospect of going to work each day filled me with dread. The work which was interesting and meaningful had become stressful and monotonous.

I could'nt sleep at night due to the stress and thought of having to go into work the next day. The stress and lack of sleep really began to negatively affect my health. The stress from the job made you irritable and cranky around my family and friends. I was no fun to be around during this time. This job was so stressful that it started to negatively affect the relationships with my loved ones. The job had zapped all the life out of me. I felt tired all the time and lacked the motivation I once had when I had joined ICICI. 

The company has no policy or no intention of ever implementing a work/life balance program for employees. Even if you apply for privilege leave you are asked by your supervisor to postpone it or cancel it altogether citing business pressures.

I don't agree with the corporate culture or the direction the Bank is headed. In my case, the culture was a turn and burn environment. They had a high pressure environment, and we lived in constant fear of losing our jobs. There was no value placed in sales professionals, and the place was a revolving door.

Your Ideas are not heard by Bosses and your work and contribution is not valued. 

The "good old boys club" at the top has made it impossible for advancement. Nobody likes to work in a situation when they know where there is no chance that they can advance within the company. If you see a pattern of upper management promoting their favorites over more qualified candidates this is a sign that your company has a "good old boys club."

At the job, I was constantly picked up, bullied and verbally abused by my boss. I refused to put up with this and there were several occasions when I confronted my Boss about it.  My former manager was a micro manager and used to call every 3 hours asking for Life Insurance numbers. I sometimes wondered if we were working for a life insurance company or a Bank. 

This manager pitted his people against one another. He was partial to some employees and harsh with others even if their numbers were the same. Office politics kill morale and as the manager you should be doing things to prevent it not perpetuate it. Don't be vindictive. Create a positive environment where people want to come to everyday.

I dont remember a single instance where my Regional Head led by example or helped me cracked a deal or a big account. In fact he used to take all the credit for the deals cracked by us.

My Boss used to curse during meetings at employees and use public humiliation to put people down. Again, do this and your employees will quit. Nobody likes someone that is a know it all and can do no wrong. Don't take all the credit when things go right and then be the first to pass blame when things go wrong. 

Have you ever had that pit in your stomach develop on Sunday just from the thought that Monday is only a day away; and, you know you have to go back to work? Well, I am glad I don't have it anymore! That place is my rear view mirror.

Some 1200 employees were laid off by the bank without giving any valid reasons with senior management saying they need to cut their losses ! 

The day I quit that job was one of the most liberating experiences in my life. I couldn't get out of there fast enough. As I was leaving, when I got to the lobby area I had a quick decision to make, take the elevator or the stairs. I quickly decided to take the stairs as I could hit them running. I didn't want to wait for the elevator! The feeling I got once I made it to my car and started to drive away was amazing. I truly felt a sense of freedom like I had never felt before!

The next few days were spent relaxing with my family and I took time out to travel abroad and preparing to move on professionally. For me quitting that poisonous job was the one of the best decisions I ever made. Today, I'm in a much better place personally. I am far less grouchy and stressed. Additionally, I am in a much better place professionally. I am also sleeping much better, and my family likes me a little more these days.

Very few pros-
I used to work in wholesale/corporate banking.
1) Largest private sector Bank in India with a finger in many pies-you get to know a little about the many pies.
2) Since the organisation is poorly run, attrition is high, the atmosphere is chaotic-it is possible for someone who is young and relatively inexperienced to handle responsibilities and gain work experience that will be valued elsewhere. Operative word is" elsewhere". Good luck getting it here
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Where do I begin?
Actually,every possible con you can have in an employer. It's a classic "kiss up and kick down" organisation with  consequential business under performance that is camouflaged under a litany of insignificant facts, half truths, convenient representation and CEO/Chairman worship.

1) Nothing in this organisation works. You have been recruited by HR as superman/batman/pokey man.  Get used to preachy(and delusional) articles written by the high ups in HR and posted on the intranet.  Btw the compensation sucks and they try to reduce it further....a minute late after 9:15 am and you lose half a day's salary. Technically, the system is designed so your boss can approve the "late-coming"..but everyone is so petrified here of "big brother HR" that it won't happen. Most people work on average considerably longer than the 5:45 pm closing time....I would estimate that in some departments a 12 hour day is average. But no-no extra salary for staying after 5:45 pm!

2) Training-what training? Nothing that will add any value to you professionally. You are sent on HR mandated training every now and then where you get to learn how to "organise yourself better" or how to "be more effective"....you get the picture. No training which you suggest will ever be approved unless it is free. Don't think, even for a moment that I am exaggerating.

3) Time off policies...haven't you got the picture by now? Officially, the leave allowance per annum is pretty generous. But good luck getting any of it. Also-no leave in the first year you join. ..that's right. 0 days.  Since the organisation is so chaotic and disorganized(  in house IT systems to save costs...cheap but don't do the job) be prepared to be bothered by phonecalls at all times. People are disturbed on weddings/funerals...it's all part of the culture of no respect for anyone unless they are high up in the hierarchy. If you are sufficiently high up enough-someone will warm your toilet seats and catch the droplets when you sneeze( lest your exalted snot be tarnished by contact with the dusty floor)

4) Leadership: Usually lacking, although there are exceptions. In general, be prepared to be thrown under the bus if anything doesn't work out even if you were not involved in the outcome but are merely associated in some way with it. Feckless, manipulative and devious would be the best description of leadership here.

5) Promotions and compensation: Are you an old timer...do you have "vintage"? Better still are you female?  If you aren't female-prepare to see reverse discrimination when it comes to performance rating. If you haven't been around for long-you need to really think about whether it is worth sticking around for better bonuses/ratings.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: DO NOT CONSIDER WORKING FOR ICICI EVER UNLESS YOU HAVE NO OTHER CHOICE!!!
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
- HR Policy set in place (though the execution and practicality is far from it)
- Digital First Approach
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
There are so many but if I have to list done some below are the top cons:
- SILO Working Culture : Everybody in the bank works in SILOS and have no idea or care what the other teams are doing
- EXCESSIVE BUREAUCRACY : Think of it more like a Sarkari Company. There are lots of instances where people don't even pick up your calls or even cared to answer to msgs or reply to mails if you are at a lower grade than them. 
- WRONG ROLE FIT : There are people who have no knowledge of what is going on or do not have any experiences on it being tasked to handle a specific role (which is not always bad and can be a learning experience). But in most cases, the person has no idea and not even to think or learn thus creating problems for the team 
- THE MORE YOU BUTTER UP YOUR BOSS, THE MORE PREFERENCE YOU GET : I have seen this first hand so many times where people who don't really know their jobs well or aren't very good are always preferred just because they keep buttering up their bosses or super bosses
- SOME BOSSES JUST DON'T CARE : Some bosses don't even care at all about their subordinates and you have to keep following up even for the smallest of approvals. Then the bosses will immediately storm you with pressures or blame you for a particular work only when asked.
- TOO MANY TEAMS FOR WORK WHICH CAN BE DONE BY ONE PERSON : I am sure this happens in all big companies (trust me I know as I have worked in 2 big MNCs before ICICI) but ICICI takes it to the next level. What can be achieved and easily handled by one person is being routed or managed by 4-5 different teams
- UNHAPPY EMPLOYEES : During my 3 years in ICICI, I have not come across a single person who are really happy with working for ICICI (unless they are a very senior person where its not that easy to switch jobs or someone who is in the bank for so long and has got accustomed to the whole culture and not really progressing in their career). TRUST ME WHEN I SAY THIS, EACH AND EVERY SINGLE PERSON I HAVE COME ACROSS ( at least the ones who wanna move ahead in life) IN ICICI ARE LOOKING OUT BUT ARE NOT ABLE TO FIND A JOB OUTSIDE due to the culture, knowledge gap and many of the other factors mentioned above and others as well. I even know friends and colleagues who are desperately looking out since 3 years but are not able to find a job elsewhere.
- ER MANAGER (WHAT DO THEY DO EXACTLY) : This is called an Employee Engagement manager who are supposed to be a helping hand for the employees and all employees are assigned an ER manager. Ask any ICICI employee and people would make fun of them(not because they are incompetent (but simply because of how they function). They would never pick up calls when their assigned employees would call them up nor barely bothered to reply to mails. I had 3-4 ER managers during my time in ICICI and I could only speak to 1 (that too just once and only after escalating it to the superior)
- SEAT ASSIGNMENT : THERE is a saying it is easier to get a seat in BKC than getting into ICICI. Why would you hire people telling them that they are based in BKC when they have to struggle for a seat for 3-5 years!!!

........ and the list goes on. You can actually check with your friends working in ICICI whether these are actually true and I am sure they can validate it.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Unless you are very desperate, think twice before joining Innovation group!

-Led by people from background in Retail Sales, Branch Ops, Call Centres etc. who don't have flair for technology
-Nothing to learn much except for increase in patience while interacting with impatient bosses
-Attrition of 70-80% - mostly people leave before completing 1 year or 6 months
-Be ready to say "Yes" to impractical solutions/ ideas
-Shouting and yelling on subordinates is a daily story
-Sub-standard hikes & late promotions
-No specific skill to showcase in market after spending some time here
-You may find bosses themselves taking internal movements from this dept to other depts in ICICI group
-If you are not a "Yes-man" to your boss, you may be witness public bashing for no reasons
-Your profile will have event management (arranging tables, chairs etc) as 50% work

Over the years ICICI Bank has grown with presence across all the Banking & Financial services areas, however their roots remain in Retail Sales, thus the mid to senior management in every dept is stuffed with elderly folks with Sales mindset, thus leading to yelling and shouting culture and poor professionalism. Achieving targets will not always run a top-heavy organization, it's the employees who will run it. In some time the young blood will start looking for greener pastures.


Sincere Advice: 
Do not join this group if you have some aspirations to learn & grow in the industry, better work in a start-up or a decent firm or wait for right opportunity!
